notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateGovernanceInstance, GetGovernanceInstance, ListGovernanceInstances, UpdateGovernanceInstance, DeleteGovernanceInstance, and ChangeGovernanceInstanceCompartment are present in v65.110.0. Create and update return a GovernanceInstance body plus opc-work-request-id; delete returns only request and work-request headers. The lifecycle enum is CREATING, ACTIVE, DELETING, DELETED, and NEEDS_ATTENTION.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a matching GovernanceInstance resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime contract stays narrower than the imported SDK surface: only displayName, description, licenseType, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. ChangeGovernanceInstanceCompartment remains out of scope, while compartmentId, tenancyNamespace, idcsAccessToken, and systemTags stay replacement-only drift.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: pre-create lookup lists by exact compartmentId plus displayName, narrows to matching licenseType summaries, then rereads each candidate through GetGovernanceInstance and reuses only a unique candidate whose tenancyNamespace also matches and whose lifecycle is ACTIVE or CREATING.

Create, update, and delete responses expose opc-work-request-id, but the SDK exposes no service-local GetWorkRequest or ListWorkRequests helpers. The reviewed generatedruntime client records request and work-request breadcrumbs when present and relies on lifecycle projection plus confirm-delete rereads rather than work-request polling.

Needs-attention behavior stays explicit in repo-authored semantics: GovernanceInstance lifecycle state NEEDS_ATTENTION is treated as a terminal failure without requeue, not as a reusable or successful steady state.
